title: Parameter names used into ArgumentException constructors should match an existing one
description: Parameter names used into ArgumentException constructors should match an existing oneAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?Some constructors of theArgumentException,ArgumentNullException,ArgumentOutOfRangeExceptionandDuplicateWaitObjectExceptionclasses must be fed with a valid parameter name. This rule raises an issue in two cases:When this parameter name doesn’t match any existing ones.When a call is made to the default (parameterless) constructorNoncompliant code examplepublic void Foo(Bar a, int[] b) { throw new ArgumentException(); // Noncompliant throw new ArgumentException("My error message", "c"); // Noncompliant throw new ArgumentException("My error message", "c", innerException); // Noncompliant throw new ArgumentNullException("c"); /
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects methods that throw ArgumentException or related exceptions - requires manual review to verify parameter name matches
logic.triggering_conditions: MethodDeclaration nodes containing ArgumentException constructor calls
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes unit tests, logging statements, generated code, and calls using nameof() operator
logic.reasoning: Detects ArgumentException constructor calls that may have invalid parameter names. This is a simplified implementation that identifies candidate methods for manual review. A complete implementation would require semantic analysis to validate that parameter names in exception constructors match actual method parameters.

should_trigger: public void Foo(Bar a) { throw new ArgumentException("error", "c"); }
public void Test(int value) { throw new ArgumentNullException("missing"); }
public void Process() { throw new ArgumentException(); }
should_not_trigger: public void Foo(Bar a) { throw new ArgumentException("error", nameof(a)); }
[Test] public void Test() { throw new ArgumentException(); }
